incorrects.description: Student selected 5 × 4, reversed the groups and items

params.description: All 4 circles highlight to show the groups.

steps.text: You reversed it. Count the circles first. That number goes first in the expression. How many circles?

incorrects.description: Student selected 4 + 5, used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: All 4 circles highlight briefly, then one circle opens to show its 5 dots.

steps.text: You used plus. When we have equal groups, we use times, not plus. You have 4 circles with 5 in each. That's 4 times 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 + 4, used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: All 4 circles highlight briefly, then one circle opens to show its 5 dots.

steps.text: You used plus. Equal groups need the times symbol. Count the groups, then count what's in each group. Which symbol connects them?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Circles highlight one by one as they are counted: 1, 2, 3, 4. Then one circle opens and its dots highlight: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

steps.text: Here's how to see it. First, I count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4. That's 4 groups. Then I look inside one circle and count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. That's 5 in each group. When I have groups with the same amount in each, I use times. 4 groups times 5 in each equals 4 × 5. The answer is 4 × 5.
